label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Doyle was surprised. The choices are A to D. Let me read through the story again to make sure I understand. So, the story goes that Sir Arthur Conan Doyle is on a trip in Europe. He gets out of the train station and into a taxi. The driver immediately addresses him by name: "Where can I take you, Mr. Doyle?" Doyle is surprised and asks if the driver has seen him before. The driver says no but explains how he figured it out using clues from the newspaper, skin color, ink spot on the fingers, and his clothes. Then the driver adds that the name was on the suitcase. The key point here is that the driver called him by his name without prior knowledge. Doyle was surprised because the driver knew his name. The driver didn't actually know him personally but deduced it through observation. So the main reason for his surprise is that the driver addressed him by name. The options are (B) the driver could call him by his name and (A) the driver had seen him before. Since the driver said he hadn't seen him before, A is incorrect. The correct answer would be B because the driver used the clues to figure out his name. The other options don't fit. D says the driver answered no questions, but the driver did explain after being asked.
